keyPoints: The web is a graph of interconnected documents (pages) and other resources, linked by URLs.
Search engines use web crawlers (spiders) to traverse and index these pages.
Information Retrieval (IR) on the web involves ranking algorithms to find the most relevant pages for a user query.
commonMistakes: Confusing the surface web (indexed by search engines) with the deep web (unindexed databases, private content) and dark web (requires specific software).
Assuming search engine results are an objective ranking of 'the best' information, rather than a product of specific algorithms and commercial factors.
